name: claude-code-diff-review
description: Per-hunk review of Claude Code session changes with in-context AI chat. Pro/Max OAuth and API-key auth supported.

commands.title: Claude Review: Switch Install Scope (user ↔ workspace)
Claude Review: Set Anthropic API Key
Claude Review: Clear Anthropic API Key
Claude Review: Set Claude OAuth Token (Max plan)
Claude Review: Clear Claude OAuth Token
Claude Review: Use Claude Code Auth (probe & report)

claudeReview.installScope.description: Where to install Claude Code hooks. `user` (default) writes to ~/.claude/settings.json so every project picks them up automatically. `workspace` writes to <workspace>/.claude/settings.json — per-project. Use the 'Switch Install Scope' command to toggle.

claudeReview.history.enabled.description: Persist every Claude Code turn (started, stopped, hunk decisions) to a content-addressed event log. Powers the History panel and crash recovery. Disable to opt out entirely; existing logs are not deleted.

claudeReview.history.retentionDays.description: Number of days to retain Memory Design event log sessions. The retention sweeper removes older sessions + their unreferenced blobs every 10 minutes.

claudeReview.crashRecoveryToast.enabled.description: Deprecated in v0.3 — superseded by the status-bar pending-reviews indicator. When true, surfaces a one-time toast on activation if any session has an open turn within the last 7 days. The status bar covers a strict superset of this case; the toast is retained for one release to ease the v0.2 → v0.3 transition. Default will flip to false in v0.4.

claudeReview.chat.transcriptContext.description: Inject Claude Code's transcript context (the user's original prompt + Claude's surrounding tool calls) into hunk chat queries. Off → chat sees only the hunk diff. Transcript content is read host-side only and never forwarded to the webview.

claudeReview.autoOpenPanel.description: Open the review panel automatically when Claude Code finishes a turn.

claudeReview.chatModel.description: Anthropic model used for hunk chat. Haiku is fast and cheap and a good fit for explaining a single hunk; switch to a larger model (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-7) for deeper reviews.

claudeReview.chatMaxTokens.description: Maximum output tokens per chat response.

claudeReview.maxSessionBytes.description: Maximum bytes of original-file snapshots retained per session.

claudeReview.telemetry.description: Opt in to anonymous usage telemetry. Also gated by VS Code's global telemetry setting.

scripts.release:check: npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test && npm run audit:licenses && npm run build
